query: CDC VSP cruise ship gastrointestinal illness outbreaks seasonal pattern monthly distribution 2023 2024 2025
answer:
# CDC VSP Cruise Ship Gastrointestinal Illness Outbreaks: Seasonal & Monthly Distribution (2023–2025)

## Overview

The CDC's Vessel Sanitation Program (VSP) tracks gastrointestinal illness (GI) outbreaks on cruise ships under U.S. jurisdiction. An outbreak is reported when ≥3% of passengers or crew report GI symptoms. Across 2023–2025, the VSP recorded **55 total outbreaks**, with a clear upward annual trend and a strong winter-dominated seasonal pattern driven overwhelmingly by norovirus.

---

## Annual Totals & Trend

- **2023**: 14 outbreaks
- **2024**: 18 outbreaks (+29% year-over-year)
- **2025**: 23 outbreaks (+28% year-over-year)

The compound annual growth rate is ~28%, likely reflecting increased cruise passenger volumes post-pandemic and, in 2024–2025, an especially virulent norovirus season driven by the novel GII.17[P17] strain.

---

## Monthly Distribution (by voyage start date)

| Month | 2023 | 2024 | 2025 | 3-Year Total |
|-------|:----:|:----:|:----:|:------------:|
| January | 3 | 1 | 6 | **10** |
| February | 1 | 1 | 4 | **6** |
| March | 6 | 1 | 3 | **10** |
| April | 0 | 2 | 4 | **6** |
| May | 2 | 2 | 0 | **4** |
| June | 1 | 0 | 0 | **1** |
| July | 0 | 0 | 1 | **1** |
| August | 0 | 0 | 0 | **0** |
| September | 0 | 2 | 1 | **3** |
| October | 1 | 1 | 1 | **3** |
| November | 0 | 0 | 1 | **1** |
| December | 0 | 6 | 2 | **8** |

**Peak months**: January and March (10 outbreaks each), followed by December (8).
**Trough**: June–August combined account for just 2 outbreaks total.

---

## Seasonal/Quarterly Pattern

| Quarter | Total Outbreaks | Share |
|---------|:--------------:|:-----:|
| Q1 (Jan–Mar) | 26 | **47%** |
| Q2 (Apr–Jun) | 11 | **20%** |
| Q3 (Jul–Sep) | 4 | **7%** |
| Q4 (Oct–Dec) | 14 | **25%** |

| Season | Total Outbreaks | Share |
|--------|:--------------:|:-----:|
| Winter (Dec–Feb) | 24 | **44%** |
| Spring (Mar–May) | 20 | **36%** |
| Summer (Jun–Aug) | 2 | **4%** |
| Fall (Sep–Nov) | 9 | **16%** |

**Key pattern**: ~80% of outbreaks occur in the winter-spring period (December–May). Summer is nearly outbreak-free. A modest fall uptick in September–October precedes the major winter peak, consistent with the start of the annual norovirus season on land.

---

## Pathogen Breakdown (2023–2025 Combined)

| Agent | Count | Share |
|-------|:-----:|:-----:|
| **Norovirus** | **46** | **84%** |
| E. coli | 2 | 4% |
| Salmonella | 1 | 2% |
| Salmonella + E. coli | 1 | 2% |
| Ciguatera (presumed) | 1 | 2% |
| Unknown | 4 | 7% |

Norovirus overwhelmingly dominates. Among outbreaks with an identified pathogen, norovirus accounts for **90%**. The seasonal pattern of cruise ship outbreaks closely mirrors land-based norovirus surveillance (CDC NoroSTAT).

---

## Cruise Line Frequency (2023–2025)

| Cruise Line | Total Outbreaks |
|-------------|:--------------:|
| Holland America | 11 |
| Royal Caribbean | 10 |
| Princess Cruises | 8 |
| Celebrity Cruises | 6 |
| Cunard Line | 4 |
| Viking (all brands) | 3 |
| P&O Cruises | 2 |
| Silversea | 2 |
| Others | 9 |

Holland America, Royal Caribbean, and Princess Cruises collectively account for 53% of all reported outbreaks.

---

## Notable Observations

1. **December 2024 was exceptionally active** with 6 outbreaks, including the Queen Mary 2 sailing (12/21–1/3) that saw a 13.5% passenger attack rate (346/2,565 ill) — the highest single-voyage rate in the dataset.
2. **The 2024–2025 winter season was the most severe** in the 3-year window, consistent with the emergence of the GII.17[P17] norovirus strain.
3. **Data limitations**: The ≥3% reporting threshold means many smaller outbreaks go unreported; actual GI illness events are substantially higher.

---

key_facts: CDC VSP recorded 55 total cruise ship GI outbreaks across 2023-2025: 14 in 2023, 18 in 2024, and 23 in 2025, showing a ~28% compound annual growth rate.
January and March are the peak outbreak months, each with 10 outbreaks over the 3-year period; December follows with 8.
Q1 (Jan-Mar) accounts for 47% of all outbreaks; Q3 (Jul-Sep) accounts for only 7%. Summer (Jun-Aug) had just 2 outbreaks total.
Winter-spring period (Dec-May) concentrates ~80% of all outbreaks, closely mirroring land-based norovirus seasonality.
Norovirus is the causative agent in 84% of all outbreaks (46/55); among outbreaks with identified pathogens, norovirus accounts for 90%.
December 2024 was exceptionally active with 6 outbreaks, including the Queen Mary 2 voyage with a 13.5% passenger attack rate (346/2,565 ill).
Holland America (11), Royal Caribbean (10), and Princess Cruises (8) account for 53% of all reported outbreaks across 2023-2025.
The 2024-2025 winter season severity is consistent with the emergence of the novel GII.17[P17] norovirus strain.
The CDC VSP reporting threshold is ≥3% of passengers or crew reporting GI symptoms; smaller outbreaks go unreported.
August is the only month with zero outbreaks recorded across all three years.
